pubmed_links: [Nogueira et al. (2013) Novel TTC19 mutation in a family with severe psychiatric manifestations and complex III deficiency](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23532514/); [Ghezzi et al. (2015) Mutations in TTC19: expanding the molecular, clinical and biochemical phenotype](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25887401/); [Ardissone et al. (2019) A Novel TTC19 Mutation in a Patient With Neurological, Psychological, and Gastrointestinal Impairment](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31551910/)
summary: Biallelic mutations in TTC19 cause mitochondrial complex III deficiency with prominent neuropsychiatric features including progressive psychosis, spinocerebellar ataxia, and developmental regression; psychiatric manifestations such as psychosis are frequent initial presentations. (emerging evidence)
